entry.title: How Organizations Can Use Mistakes to Level Up Their Security Programs
entry.content:
Organizations repeatedly expose ports, reuse passwords, and skip patches, creating security gaps that attackers exploit for breaches. An industry veteran outlines ways to fix these common mistakes.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Critical Flaw in Langflow AI Platform Under Attack
entry.content:
Threats actors pounced on the code injection vulnerability within hours of its disclosure, demonstrating that organizations have little time to address critical bugs.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Automotive Cybersecurity Threats Grow in Era of Connected, Autonomous Vehicles
entry.content:
More than a decade since the 2015 Jeep hack, the cybersecurity of vehicles remains of the utmost importance.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Coruna, DarkSword & Democratizing Nation-State Exploit Kits
entry.content:
Nation-state malware is being sold on the Dark Web and leaked to GitHub; and ordinary organizations might not stand much of a chance of defending themselves.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Infrastructure Attacks With Physical Consequences Down 25%
entry.content:
Operational technology (OT) at industrial and critical infrastructure sites seem to have been benefitting from a lull in ransomware, and hackers' relative ignorance of OT systems.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Wartime Usage of Compromised IP Cameras Highlight Their Danger
entry.content:
The list of countries exploiting Internet-connected cameras to give them eyes inside their adversaries' borders continues to expand. What should companies look out for?
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: F5 BIG-IP Vulnerability Reclassified as RCE, Under Exploitation
entry.content:
CVE-2025-53521 was initially disclosed in October as a high-severity denial-of-service (DoS) flaw, but new information has revealed the bug is actually much more dangerous.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: AI-Powered 'DeepLoad' Malware Steals Credentials, Evades Detection
entry.content:
The massive amount of junk code that hides the malware's logic from security scans was almost certainly generated by AI, researchers say.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: FBI Confirms Kash Patel Email Hack as US Offers $10M Reward for Hackers
entry.content:
The agency said Iranian hackers targeted the director’s personal email account and noted that the compromised information is old. The post FBI Confirms Kash Patel Email Hack as US Offers $10M Reward for Hackers appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Exploitation of Fresh Citrix NetScaler Vulnerability Begins
entry.content:
The critical-severity flaw leaks application memory and can be exploited to obtain authenticated administrative session IDs. The post Exploitation of Fresh Citrix NetScaler Vulnerability Begins appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Telnyx Targeted in Growing TeamPCP Supply Chain Attack
entry.content:
Two malicious versions of the popular SDK were uploaded to the PyPI registry, targeting Windows, macOS, and Linux. The post Telnyx Targeted in Growing TeamPCP Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Hacked Hospitals, Hidden Spyware: Iran Conflict Shows How Digital Fight Is Ingrained in Warfare
entry.content:
Iran-linked hacking groups are turning to high-volume, low-impact cyberattacks, and AI is providing a boost. The post Hacked Hospitals, Hidden Spyware: Iran Conflict Shows How Digital Fight Is Ingrained in Warfare appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: European Commission Reports Cyber Intrusion and Data Theft
entry.content:
The ShinyHunters hacker group claimed to have stolen over 350GB of information from European Commission cloud systems. The post European Commission Reports Cyber Intrusion and Data Theft appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Russian APT Star Blizzard Adopts DarkSword iOS Exploit Kit
entry.content:
The state-sponsored group’s campaign has targeted government, higher education, financial, and legal entities, as well as think tanks. The post Russian APT Star Blizzard Adopts DarkSword iOS Exploit Kit appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Healthcare IT Platform CareCloud Probing Potential Data Breach
entry.content:
The company has disclosed a cybersecurity incident involving one of its electronic health record environments. The post Healthcare IT Platform CareCloud Probing Potential Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Critical Vulnerability in OpenAI Codex Allowed GitHub Token Compromise
entry.content:
Researchers found an OpenAI Codex vulnerability that could have been exploited to compromise GitHub tokens. The post Critical Vulnerability in OpenAI Codex Allowed GitHub Token Compromise appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: State Department reissues $10 million reward for info on Iranian hackers
entry.content:
Hours after the FBI confirmed that Iranian hackers gained access to Director Kash Patel’s personal email account, the State Department offered a reward for information on cyber actors within the country.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Healthcare software firm CareCloud informs SEC of potential patient data leak
entry.content:
The healthcare software firm CareCloud warned the Securities and Exchange Commission that a cyberattack may have resulted in the leak of patient data.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: European Commission downplays ShinyHunters cyberattack impact
entry.content:
In a statement issued Friday, the Commission said it had detected an incident affecting the Europa.eu web portal, the European Union’s central online platform hosting websites and services for its institutions.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Italian regulator fines financial giant $36 million for data protection failures
entry.content:
The Italian Data Protection Authority fined Intesa Sanpaolo SpA for what it called “serious shortcomings in personal data security, due to the inadequacy of the technical and organizational measures adopted.”
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
entry.content:
Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-as-a-service offering lets customers sidestep both of these pitfalls: It uses cleverly disguised links to load the target brand's real website, and then acts as a relay between the target and the legitimate site -- forwarding the victim's username, password and multi-factor authentic
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
entry.content:
AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and n
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
entry.content:
A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
entry.content:
The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
entry.content:
A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: LeakBase’s “Chucky” detained in Russia
entry.content:
TASS reports: Police have detained a Taganrog resident suspected of administering LeakBase, one of the largest hacker platforms. Law enforcement officials told TASS. The detained Taganrog resident is suspected of administering “one of the largest international hacker platforms, LeakBase,” the agency’s source said. According to him, the liquidated platform operated a credit system and user... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Meet Claude Mythos: Leaked Anthropic post reveals the powerful upcoming model
entry.content:
Matt Binder reports: An accidental leak has now been officially confirmed by AI company Anthropic regarding its most powerful AI model yet. The model, now known as “Claude Mythos,” was originally uncovered in a report from Fortune. Anthropic has since confirmed the details about the leak to the outlet. The data leak included details about the upcoming release of the... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Thousands of Corewell Health patients affected by security breach
entry.content:
Fox2 reports: Corewell Health announced their former health care consulting vendor experienced a data breach in 2024, affecting thousands of patients. Pinnacle Holdings previously provided health care consulting services to Corewell Health. After being notified of the data breach, Corewell said they conducted a review to identify who was impacted. The health system said around... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Woodfords Family Services notifying patients and families about 2024 ransomware attack
entry.content:
A notice by Woodfords Family Services in Maine caught my eye because the name sounded familiar. They provide support services for people with disabilities and their families. On March 27, 2026, they issued a notice: What Happened? On April 8, 2024, we discovered suspicious activity within our network. We took steps to secure our environment and... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Iran-linked group Handala hacked FBI Director Kash Patel’s personal email account
entry.content:
Pierluigi Paganini reports: Iran-linked hacking group Handala claims it breached FBI Director Kash Patel’s personal Gmail account and shared alleged data, including photos and files. The FBI confirmed it is aware of the incident and has taken steps to mitigate risks, stressing that the exposed material is old and does not involve any government or classified information.... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Thankfully, the Infinite Campus incident did not involve a lot of non-directory student information
entry.content:
In the wake of the Infinite Campus data breach, DataBreaches was contacted by several concerned EdTech professionals who weren’t prepared to accept Infinite Campus’s word that there was no sensitive student information in the data tranche. With their encouragement, DataBreaches downloaded the data tranche from ShinyHunters’ leak site and examined it. Most of the files... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: CareCloud notifies the SEC after attack on one of its EHR environments
entry.content:
DysruptionHub reports: CareCloud said an unauthorized third party briefly disrupted one of its six electronic health record environments on March 16, restoring access that evening as investigators assess possible patient data exposure. In a March 27 SEC filing, the Somerset, New Jersey-based company said an unauthorized third party temporarily accessed part of its CareCloud Health division... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Did you sign up for the new White House app? Don’t use it until you read this!
entry.content:
Did you sign up for the new White House app? Don’t use it until you read this, because it puts your privacy and data security at risk. Patrick Quirk takes an impressive technical piece and distills it for those of us who are not developers or coders. His article is based on original research by... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: South African government agency and Spanish psychological software provider victims of cyberattacks by XP95
entry.content:
Jan Vermeulen reports: Statistics South Africa has become the latest government entity to fall victim to a ransomware attack by the emerging cybercrime group known as XP95. The threat actors claim to have successfully breached the agency responsible for conducting South Africa’s census, as well as producing and disseminating other official statistics, like the Consumer... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Estonian hospital sends patient home with other peoples’ health data
entry.content:
Märten Hallismaa reports: A person who underwent X-ray imaging at West Tallinn Central Hospital (LTKH) was sent home with a USB drive that also contained the personal and health data of other patients. ERR has received information about a person who went to West Tallinn Central Hospital to undergo an X-ray. Because the images needed... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Deaconess Health System Affected by Vendor Data Breach
entry.content:
Evansville, Indiana-based Deaconess Health System has announced a data breach involving information shared with a third-party vendor, the MRO Corp-owned […] The post Deaconess Health System Affected by Vendor Data Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: BakerHostetler: Healthcare Remains Most Targeted Sector with Extortion-Only Attacks on the Rise
entry.content:
Healthcare has retained its position as the industry most targeted by cyber actors, an unwanted accolade that the sector has […] The post BakerHostetler: Healthcare Remains Most Targeted Sector with Extortion-Only Attacks on the Rise appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Excelsior Orthopaedics; Buffalo Surgery Center Pay $2.4 Million to Settle Data Breach Lawsuit
entry.content:
A settlement has been reached to resolve class action data breach litigation against Excelsior Orthopaedics and Buffalo Surgery Center. The […] The post Excelsior Orthopaedics; Buffalo Surgery Center Pay $2.4 Million to Settle Data Breach Lawsuit appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Data Breaches Reported by New York & Texas Plastic Surgery Practices
entry.content:
Data breaches have recently been reported by Vantage Plastic Surgery in New York City and Austin Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery […] The post Data Breaches Reported by New York & Texas Plastic Surgery Practices appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Six New Healthcare Data Breaches Announced
entry.content:
Data breaches have been announced by New Horizons Behavioral Health in Georgia, CWA Local 1180 in New York, Coastal Carolina […] The post Six New Healthcare Data Breaches Announced appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Free Webinar Tomorrow: HIPAA Email Security 101: PHI, Encryption, and What’s Required
entry.content:
According to the Paubox 2026 Healthcare Email Security Report, in 2025, 170 email-related data breaches were reported to the HHS’ […] The post Free Webinar Tomorrow: HIPAA Email Security 101: PHI, Encryption, and What’s Required appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Healthcare Software Company Announces Breach of its Electronic Health Record Environment
entry.content:
The Somerset, New Jersey-based healthcare software company CareCloud has notified the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) about a security […] The post Healthcare Software Company Announces Breach of its Electronic Health Record Environment appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Woodfords Family Services Notifies Patients Affected by April 2024 Ransomware Attack
entry.content:
Westbrook, Maine-based Woodfords Family Services, a provider of services to individuals with special needs and their families, has notified the […] The post Woodfords Family Services Notifies Patients Affected by April 2024 Ransomware Attack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: FBI confirms hack of Director Patel's personal email inbox
entry.content:
The Handala hackers associated with Iran have breached the personal email account of FBI Director Kash Patel and published photos and documents. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: European Commission confirms data breach after Europa.eu hack
entry.content:
The European Commission has confirmed a data breach after its Europa.eu web platform was hacked in a cyberattack claimed by the ShinyHunters extortion gang. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Critical Fortinet Forticlient EMS flaw now exploited in attacks
entry.content:
Attackers are now actively exploiting a critical vulnerability in Fortinet's FortiClient EMS platform, according to threat intelligence company Defused. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Hackers exploiting critical F5 BIG-IP flaw in attacks, patch now
entry.content:
F5 has reclassified a BIG-IP APM denial-of-service (DoS) vulnerability as a critical-severity remote code execution (RCE) flaw, warning that attackers are exploiting it to deploy webshells on unpatched devices. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Apple adds macOS Terminal warning to block ClickFix attacks
entry.content:
Apple has introduced a security feature in macOS Tahoe 26.4 that blocks pasting and executing potentially harmful commands in Terminal and alerts users to possible risks. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Critical Citrix NetScaler memory flaw actively exploited in attacks
entry.content:
Hackers are exploiting a critical severity vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-3055, in Citrix NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway appliances to obtain sensitive data. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: New RoadK1ll WebSocket implant used to pivot on breached networks
entry.content:
A newly identified malicious implant named RoadK1ll is enabling threat actors to quietly move from a compromised host to other systems on the network. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Healthcare tech firm CareCloud says hackers stole patient data
entry.content:
Healthcare IT firm CareCloud has disclosed a data breach incident that exposed sensitive data and caused a network disruption lasting approximately eight hours. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Unknown
entry.content:
Unknown reported a healthcare breach.
📰 Source: HHS OCR | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach
